title: echinopsis eyriesii var. schelhasii
intro: Echinopsis eyriesii var. schelhasii, also known as the Schelhas' echinopsis, is a rare and unique cactus species native to the Andes mountains in South America. It belongs to the Echinopsis genus, which is a type of cactus known for its medicinal and psychoactive properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including pain, inflammation, and fever, due to its high concentration of alkaloids, particularly mescaline. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to create teas, infusions, and other remedies that are believed to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and improve sleep quality.
